key_insight: A large language model, verified in Lean 4, completed a machine-checked proof of the FGG QAOA conjecture that depth-p QAOA on the ring of disagrees achieves approximation ratio (2p+1)/(2p+2) exactly.
engineering_relevance: This shows that AI-assisted theorem proving can turn open quantum information conjectures into formally verified results, improving trust in mathematical methods used in algorithm design and analysis.
method: Formalized the QAOA problem and existing theory in a Lean 4 quantum information library.
Reduced the conjecture to one remaining open mathematical statement.
Used an LLM plus an agentic toolkit in a feedback loop with Lean to construct and verify the proof.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports an end-to-end machine-verified proof in Lean 4, with the model uncovering a hidden dynamical symmetry and producing the certified proof.
watch_item: The approach still depends on human judgment to ensure the formal statement faithfully captures the intended conjecture, and scaling such proofs may require substantial library and tooling support.
paper_title: A Machine-Verified Proof of a Quantum-Optimization Conjecture
